name: The Denture Refugee
context: Retired or semi-retired, 60–78, has worn dentures for years and hates them. Has adapted their entire life around limitations — what they eat, how they laugh, social situations. Assumes implants are too expensive or that they're not a candidate due to bone loss. Something recently pushed them over the edge: a broken denture, an upcoming family event, a friend who got implants. Direct acknowledgment of denture frustrations, reassurance that bone loss doesn't automatically disqualify them, and visible financing options convert them.
